mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates lower the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of instances where unemployment exceeds 6.0 percent, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery that lifts corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5 percent monthly in a late-cycle expansion confirms capacity utilization strength that supports near-term equity momentum and positive short-term returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth above 0.5 percent monthly fails to predict positive 5-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with a negative or insignificant regression coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2 percent annualized confirms macroeconomic expansion that sustains corporate profit margins, driving positive forward equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth above 2 percent annualized consistently coincides with negative 60-day forward returns, or the predictive Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index breaching 220 triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 220 fail to predict negative 20-day returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy falling below 48 percent or Rank IC turning positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that boosts corporate revenue forecasts and yields positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between payrolls growth and unemployment decline shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in predicting 20-day returns, or directional accuracy drops below 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts declining by more than 5 percent month-over-month during a tightening cycle signals credit contraction and reduced consumer leverage, causing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines exceeding 5 percent monthly during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a positive or insignificant predictive coefficient.
